specific_location: Bear Creek Lake Park just east of Fitness Station #1
description: It had rained the night before and everything was wet. The two sparrows were sitting at the base of a bush soaking in the sunlight. They were sitting with their sides toward me. Both were very similiar to each other and seemed small, slim, and colorful for sparrows. I could see the streaked, dark brown crown with a pale stripe, buffy supercilium, brown ear coverts with a dark brown mustache, distinct malar stripe. The brown wings had some beige. Chin was white and sides were beige or buffy.Behaviors: The birds weere sitting at the base of a bush in a brushy area soaking in the morning sun. they were there several minutes allowing good looks.Call: n/aPlumage: adult non-breeding
